dresses.name: Himachali Topi & Kurta
dresses.description: The iconic Himachali men's traditional attire featuring the distinctive Himachali Topi (cap) adorned with vibrant patterns, paired with a handwoven woolen kurta. A symbol of pride and identity in the hill communities.
dresses.cultural_significance: The Himachali cap (Topi) is more than headwear — it represents the wearer's region, community, and status. Worn with pride during festivals like Kullu Dussehra.
dresses.fun_fact: The Kullu cap's pattern can tell you exactly which valley in Himachal the wearer belongs to!

dresses.name: Himachali Dhatu Ensemble
dresses.description: A traditionally styled ensemble featuring layered woolen garments with intricate embroidery, representing the mountain heritage of Himachal Pradesh. Includes the classic Pahadi shawl draped gracefully.
dresses.cultural_significance: The layered garment style developed as a response to the harsh mountain climate, becoming an integral part of Himachali cultural identity over centuries.
dresses.fun_fact: Himachali weavers use a traditional loom called 'Khadi' that has been in use for over 500 years!

dresses.name: Himachali Dhaalu
dresses.description: A traditional flowing dress of Himachali women, made from handwoven wool. Features vibrant geometric patterns and is paired with intricate silver jewelry and a distinctive headscarf called 'Dhaatu'.
dresses.cultural_significance: Worn during village festivals, weddings, and the famous Kullu Dussehra. Represents the rich textile heritage of the hill communities.
dresses.fun_fact: The Kullu shawl that accompanies this dress was granted a GI (Geographical Indication) tag, making it a protected heritage craft.

dresses.name: Himachali Chamba Rumal
dresses.description: An elegant traditional outfit featuring the famous Chamba Rumal embroidery — miniature paintings recreated in thread. The flowing silhouette honors centuries of Pahadi artistic tradition.
dresses.cultural_significance: Chamba Rumal embroidery style dates back to the Mughal era and depicts scenes from epics like Ramayana and Mahabharata.
dresses.fun_fact: A single Chamba Rumal can take artisans up to 2 years to complete due to the intricate embroidery work!

dresses.name: Himachali Pattoo Outfit
dresses.description: The beautiful Pattoo — a handwoven rectangular shawl worn as a wraparound garment by Himachali women, paired with traditional jewelry. Each thread carries centuries of mountain artistry.
dresses.cultural_significance: The Pattoo is traditionally woven by women during winter months, making it both a practical garment and a labor of love.
dresses.fun_fact: The wool used in traditional Himachali garments comes from Gaddi sheep that graze at altitudes above 3,000 meters!

dresses.name: Punjabi Kurta Pajama & Pagri
dresses.description: The classic Punjabi men's outfit featuring a loose-fitting Kurta (tunic), Pajama (trousers), and the magnificent Pagri (turban) tied with pride — the ultimate symbol of Punjabi identity.
dresses.cultural_significance: The Punjabi turban (Pagri/Dastar) represents honor, self-respect, courage, and piety in Sikh and Punjabi culture.
dresses.fun_fact: A traditional Punjabi turban cloth can be up to 6 meters long and comes in every color imaginable — each color has its own significance.

dresses.name: Punjabi Sherwani
dresses.description: A regal Punjabi Sherwani — the epitome of masculine grace in Punjabi wedding culture. Rich embroidery, intricate button work, and a commanding silhouette define this iconic outfit.
dresses.cultural_significance: The Sherwani became the go-to groom's outfit in Punjab, blending Mughal elegance with Punjabi flamboyance.
dresses.fun_fact: A Punjabi wedding Sherwani can have over 50,000 individual hand-stitched beads!

dresses.name: Phulkari Salwar Kameez
dresses.description: The quintessential Punjabi women's outfit — a flowing Kameez (tunic) paired with Salwar (trousers) and a vibrant Phulkari dupatta embroidered with floral motifs.
dresses.cultural_significance: Phulkari literally means 'flower work'. This embroidery tradition dates back centuries and is passed down through generations of Punjabi women.
dresses.fun_fact: A single Phulkari dupatta can take 2-3 months to embroider by hand, with each stitch telling a story of Punjabi heritage.

dresses.name: Punjabi Patiala Suit
dresses.description: The iconic Patiala Salwar — with its dramatic pleated volume paired with a short Kameez and flowing dupatta. Named after the royal city of Patiala, this is Punjabi fashion at its finest.
dresses.cultural_significance: The Patiala Salwar was inspired by the royal court of Patiala and became a worldwide fashion phenomenon representing Punjabi style.
dresses.fun_fact: The Patiala Salwar uses almost twice the fabric of a regular salwar due to its signature voluminous pleats!

dresses.name: Rajasthani Angarkha & Pagari
dresses.description: The majestic Rajasthani Angarkha — an overlapping front-tied tunic paired with the iconic Rajasthani Pagari (turban) in vivid colors. A look fit for the royal courts of Rajputana.
dresses.cultural_significance: The Rajasthani turban's color, size, and style indicate the wearer's caste, region, and occasion. It's a walking cultural encyclopedia.
dresses.fun_fact: The Rajasthani Pagari can be up to 9 meters long — some festival turbans weigh over 15 kg!

dresses.name: Rajasthani Dhoti Kurta
dresses.description: Traditional Rajasthani men's Dhoti Kurta with intricate mirror work and block printing. The outfit represents the vibrant desert culture with its rich colors and detailed craftsmanship.
dresses.cultural_significance: Block printing on these garments uses techniques from the royal ateliers of Jaipur and Jodhpur, unchanged for 500 years.
dresses.fabric: Cotton with block print & mirror work
dresses.fun_fact: Rajasthani block printing artisans carve wooden blocks with over 1,000 tiny details for a single pattern!

dresses.name: Rajasthani Jodhpuri Suit
dresses.description: The legendary Jodhpuri suit (Bandhgala) — born in the princely state of Jodhpur and now a global fashion icon. Tailored to perfection with a closed-neck jacket and fitted trousers.
dresses.cultural_significance: Created for Maharaja Pratap Singh of Jodhpur, this suit became a favorite of British royalty and eventually a global menswear staple.
dresses.fun_fact: The Jodhpuri suit inspired the riding breeches called 'jodhpurs' — worn by equestrians worldwide!

dresses.name: Rajasthani Folk Attire
dresses.description: Authentic Rajasthani folk attire featuring traditional embroidery, vibrant Rajwadi patterns, and the classic desert warrior aesthetic. A symphony of color against golden sands.
dresses.cultural_significance: Folk attire varies dramatically across Rajasthan's 33 districts, each carrying the identity of its community.
dresses.fun_fact: Rajasthan's textile market is worth over ₹30,000 crore — the largest traditional textile economy in India!

dresses.name: Ghagra Choli & Odhni
dresses.description: A stunning three-piece outfit consisting of a flared Ghagra (skirt) with mirror work, a fitted Choli (blouse), and a flowing Odhni (veil) with Bandhani tie-dye patterns.
dresses.cultural_significance: This outfit embodies the vibrant spirit of Rajasthan. The mirror work (Shisha) is believed to ward off evil spirits, while Bandhani represents celebration.
dresses.fabric: Cotton, Silk with Mirror work & Bandhani
dresses.colors: Fiery Red, Royal Blue, Mustard Yellow, Hot Pink
dresses.fun_fact: A traditional Rajasthani Ghagra can have up to 15 meters of fabric in its flare, creating a magnificent swirl when the wearer dances.

dresses.name: Rajasthani Bandhani Saree
dresses.description: The iconic Rajasthani Bandhani (tie-dye) saree — a canvas of thousands of tiny knots creating mesmerizing patterns. Each dot is hand-tied, making every saree a unique masterpiece.
dresses.cultural_significance: Bandhani is considered auspicious and is an essential part of a Rajasthani bride's trousseau. The art is over 5,000 years old.
dresses.colors: Red, Yellow with white dot patterns
dresses.fun_fact: A master Bandhani artist can tie up to 10,000 knots in a single day — that's one knot every 3 seconds!

dresses.name: Rajasthani Leheriya Outfit
dresses.description: The vibrant Leheriya (wave) print — a tie-dye technique producing colorful diagonal striped patterns that ripple like desert waves. A monsoon festival favorite.
dresses.cultural_significance: Leheriya is traditionally worn during Teej and the monsoon season. The wave pattern symbolizes the joy of rains in arid Rajasthan.
dresses.colors: Multi-color waves — Pink, Green, Yellow, Orange
dresses.fun_fact: Creating a Leheriya print requires rolling the fabric diagonally and tying it at intervals — a technique unchanged since the 18th century!

dresses.name: Rajasthani Gota Patti Ensemble
dresses.description: A regal ensemble adorned with Gota Patti — golden ribbon appliqué work that catches light like desert stars. Rajasthani royalty at its sparkling finest.
dresses.cultural_significance: Gota Patti originated in the royal courts of Rajputana. The golden work represents prosperity and is considered essential for bridal attire.
dresses.fabric: Silk with Gota Patti & Zari work
dresses.fun_fact: Authentic Gota Patti uses real gold-plated copper ribbons — a single bridal outfit can use over 200 meters of Gota!
